reviews.review_title: Really fun until the museum level
reviews.review_description: I've wanted this game ever since I got my vr headset and it is extremely fun but once you get to the museum level prepare to get absolutely Infuriated by how many times you'll die

Pros
The throwing mechanics feel intuitive and grounded.
Physical interaction adds a strong sense of immersion.
The physics system keeps gameplay engaging and unpredictable.
A satisfying way to unwind and release stress after a long day.

Cons
The game is more physically demanding than expected. Playing while seated is difficult; only experienced players might manage it later on.
You need a clear space of at least a 1.5-meter radius. It’s very easy to hit and potentially break nearby objects.
The difficulty spike in the final chapter feels excessive and may frustrate players rather than challenge them.

This game is really good because you just tossed things down at building on different maps and it’s really cool how you get to see the background and you get to throw even the people that you’re with off the building to hit zombies and I like the creations of the bosses￼

reviews.review_title: Very expensive for what it is.
reviews.review_description:
Amazing how a video can make a game look really good. This is very simplistic and is not worthy of the £12.99 asking price.

However, young kids may enjoy this but the price needs reducing drastically!!

It's oddly addictive and fun.  But there's a problem with this version.  I with there was a better system for room adjustment so I don't accidentally press literally any controller button and fall out the window.

I found it silly fun! I loved it!
Great game for all ages!
Easy enough to learn, yet still a challenge!
Zombies are climbing up after you and you need to throw down at them whatever you find from behind you. Some zombies are easier to knock down than others. I find it a thrill! This game is simple and a great time!
Its overall good. Odd price at the very least but still fun/enjoyable.
I’ve been wanting this game since I was young now I have a VR headset I decided to buy it the price was quite odd but the game is worth it 100%

A lot of reviews say it is overpriced or to simple, but I like the arcade style simplicity and the resource management part of it. One part of it is the broken achievements. I got backstabbed by a zombie and got the achievement for throwing a grenade without pulling the pin, I got the achievement for beating level 5 with the NPC when replaying level 3 to find the magic piece thing, I even got the achievement for beating the not-so-secret-anymore 6th level of the game for I don’t even know what. And that in LESS than 3 hours of gameplay
